name: context-window-management
description: Strategies for managing LLM context windows including summarization, trimming, routing, and avoiding context rot Use when: context window, token limit, context management, context engineering, long context.
capabilities: Apply tiered context strategies based on conversation size and complexity
Implement serial position optimization to place critical information at context boundaries
Summarize conversation history by importance rather than chronological order
Route different query types to appropriate context handling strategies
Count and track token usage to prevent context window overflow
Prioritize high-value context over low-value verbose content
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static analyzer findings are false positives. The backtick characters at line 54 are Markdown inline code formatting for skill references, not shell command execution. No cryptographic algorithms exist in the document. This is a documentation-only skill providing educational guidance on context window management with no executable code, external commands, or network access.
valueStatement: Long conversations and complex prompts overwhelm LLMs, causing lost information and poor responses. This skill teaches strategic context curation, token optimization, and intelligent summarization to maintain high-quality AI interactions.
limitations: Cannot increase the actual token limit of the underlying LLM model
Summarization may lose nuanced details from original conversation content
Requires manual judgment to determine which context elements are most important
Does not automatically integrate with external memory or vector database systems

useCases.targetUser: Software developers working on large projects requiring extended AI collaboration
useCases.title: Long-Form Technical Documentation Assistant
useCases.description: Maintain coherent multi-session conversations about complex codebases by strategically summarizing previous discussions while preserving critical architectural decisions and implementation details

useCases.targetUser: Customer support teams using AI assistants for ticket management
useCases.title: Customer Support Conversation Management
useCases.description: Handle high-volume customer inquiries by intelligently trimming resolved issues while retaining context about ongoing problems, customer preferences, and escalation history

useCases.targetUser: Researchers and analysts conducting extended AI-assisted investigations
useCases.title: Research and Analysis Workflow
useCases.description: Synthesize insights from lengthy research sessions by prioritizing key findings and methodology notes while discarding exploratory dead-ends and redundant analysis paths

promptTemplates.title: Basic Context Summary
promptTemplates.prompt: Summarize the key points from our conversation so far. Focus on decisions made, open questions, and action items. Keep the summary under 500 tokens while preserving all critical information needed to continue our work.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when approaching token limits in a long conversation and need to preserve essential context

promptTemplates.title: Strategic Context Trimming
promptTemplates.prompt: Review our conversation history and identify which sections can be safely condensed or removed. Prioritize keeping: recent exchanges, unresolved topics, and specific technical details. Summarize or remove: brainstorming tangents, resolved issues, and generic discussions.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when you need to selectively reduce context while maintaining conversation coherence

promptTemplates.title: Serial Position Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Reorganize the following context to place the most critical information at the beginning and end. Group related topics together. Move supporting details to the middle sections. This optimizes for the serial position effect in LLM attention.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when preparing context for important decision-making or complex problem-solving sessions

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Session Context Handoff
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a context handoff document that captures: current project state, key decisions with rationale, pending tasks with priorities, relevant technical specifications, and known constraints. Format for easy insertion into a new conversation while maintaining full continuity.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when transitioning a long-running project to a new conversation session

bestPractices: Place critical instructions and constraints at the very beginning of your context to ensure they receive maximum attention from the model
Summarize conversation history periodically based on importance and relevance, not just age - some old context remains more valuable than recent exchanges
Use explicit section markers and structured formatting to help the model parse and retrieve relevant information efficiently from large contexts
antiPatterns: Appending all conversation history without curation leads to context bloat where important details get lost in verbose low-value content
Assuming the model remembers everything equally well - context position matters significantly with degraded attention to middle sections
Using uniform truncation that cuts off the most recent context, losing critical information about current task state and unresolved issues

faq.question: What happens when I exceed the context window limit?
faq.answer: The model cannot process tokens beyond its context window. Requests may fail entirely, or earlier content gets silently dropped. Proactive context management prevents this by summarizing or trimming before hitting limits.

faq.question: How do I decide what context to keep versus summarize?
faq.answer: Prioritize by importance to current tasks, not recency. Keep specific technical details, decisions with rationale, and unresolved items. Summarize brainstorming, resolved discussions, and generic conversations.

faq.question: What is the serial position effect in LLM context?
faq.answer: LLMs show stronger attention to content at the beginning and end of context windows, with degraded performance on information in the middle. Strategic placement of critical content improves model performance.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with any AI model?
faq.answer: Yes, context management principles apply universally across Claude, Codex, and other LLMs. However, specific token limits and attention patterns vary by model - check your model documentation for details.

faq.question: How often should I summarize conversation context?
faq.answer: Summarize proactively when you reach 50-75 percent of your context window, before major topic transitions, or at natural conversation checkpoints. Waiting until you are near the limit risks losing critical information.

faq.question: Does summarizing context lose important details?
faq.answer: Some detail loss is inevitable, but strategic summarization preserves essentials while removing noise. Focus on decisions, rationale, specifications, and action items. You can always retrieve full history if needed for specific details.
